sends_today.signal_text: scarboroughbayplumbing.co.uk loads over HTTP — Chrome shows 'Not Secure' warning to every visitor, including homeowners searching for an emergency plumber
sends_today.pain_point: Website lacks SSL certificate — browsers show 'Not Secure' warning
sends_today.subject: Mike — Chrome flags your Scarborough plumber site as unsafe
sends_today.email_body:
Hi Mike,

Anyone who finds scarboroughbayplumbing.co.uk right now sees a 'Not Secure' warning in their browser before they've had a chance to read about you.

For domestic plumbing, homeowners often check the website before calling — especially for bigger jobs. That warning tells them something's not right, and most move on to the next result.

SSL is free to install and usually takes about 20 minutes. I've sorted this for a couple of East Yorkshire tradespeople recently — happy to check which host you're on and send you the exact steps.

Reply and I'll take a look.

[Your name]

sends_today.signal_text: HTTP site, no mobile support — solicitors asking clients to submit enquiries via an unsecured, non-mobile-optimised page
sends_today.pain_point: Website lacks SSL certificate — browsers show 'Not Secure' warning on a site collecting client enquiries
sends_today.subject: The Old Court Solicitors — your client enquiry form has an SSL issue
sends_today.email_body:
Hi,

oldcourtsolicitors.co.uk runs over HTTP, which means Chrome shows a 'Not Secure' warning to anyone who visits — before they've had a chance to see who you are or what you do.

For a solicitors' practice, this is particularly sensitive. Prospective clients filling in enquiry forms on an unsecured page may think twice before submitting their name, contact details, or the nature of their legal matter. The ICO's GDPR guidance recommends encryption for any site collecting personal data.

An SSL certificate fixes this — it's usually free and takes about 20 minutes to install. The mobile issue is a separate but straightforward fix.

I help professional services firms sort both — happy to run a quick check on the site and send you what I find.

[Your name]
sends_today.cta: Reply and I'll run a free check on the site
sends_today.personalisation_note: No owner name — address to 'Hi' or 'Hi there'. Use business name in subject.

sends_today.pain_point: Website lacks SSL certificate — browsers show 'Not Secure' warning
sends_today.subject: Tony — Chrome flags your Scarborough roofing site as unsafe
sends_today.email_body:
Hi Tony,

Anyone who visits scarboroughroofing.co.uk sees a 'Not Secure' warning in their browser before they've read a single thing about you.

Roofing is a high-value job and homeowners take their time researching. A security warning at that point says the opposite of professional and trustworthy — and most people will click away rather than find out more.

SSL is free on most hosting plans and takes about 20 minutes to switch on. I've sorted this recently for a couple of other Yorkshire trades businesses.

Reply and I'll check which host you're on and send you the exact steps.

[Your name]

sends_today.signal_text: pocklingtonfurniture.co.uk fails the mobile test — furniture shoppers browsing on a phone cannot navigate or view products properly
sends_today.pain_point: Website not mobile-friendly — losing mobile visitors during the browsing phase
sends_today.subject: James — Pocklington Fine Furniture's website isn't mobile-friendly
sends_today.email_body:
Hi James,

Right now, pocklingtonfurniture.co.uk doesn't pass Google's mobile test — which means anyone browsing for furniture on their phone in Pocklington is getting a broken layout when they find you.

For a showroom business, the mobile site is often the first impression — people browse on their phone on the sofa, then visit in person. A site that doesn't load correctly on mobile loses you before you've had a chance to show them what you stock.

The fix is usually straightforward and doesn't require a full redesign.

Happy to run a free mobile test and send you the report — reply and I'll put it together.

[Your name]
sends_today.cta: Happy to run a free mobile test and send you the report
